title: Substance Use Disorder Counseling * Veterans - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Substance Use Disorder Counseling * Veterans 5 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter African American Community Domestic Violence Issues Ex-Offenders Hispanic/Latino Community Mothers Native American Community Pregnant Individuals Veterans Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF RETURNING VETERANS PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential mental and physical health services through volunteer health providers. Traditional and non-traditional services may be available. Eligibility: Veterans, Service Members, including National Guard and Reserve personnel, and Military Family members (including spouses/partners, children, parents, grandparents, and siblings directly impacted by military service). Hours: Varies Email (503) 954-2259 Visit Website More Details VA NORTHWEST HEALTH NETWORK PORTLAND VA MEDICAL CENTER HEALTH CARE SYSTEM SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Options for veterans seeking treatment for substance use problems, including individual and group therapy and medications to help clients reduce alcohol, tobacco, and drug use. VA providers know that, in many cases, substance use problems are continuing conditions that require care over a long period of time. For other veterans, substance use problems may be resolved more quickly with attention paid to related problems. Such related problems could be post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD), depression, pain, disturbed sleep, irritability, and/or relationship problems. 3710 SW US Veterans Hospital Road Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Call for information Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm (800) 949-8262 1004 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VA NORTHWEST HEALTH NETWORK SOUTHERN OREGON VA REHABILITATION CENTER AND CLINICS SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Options for veterans seeking treatment for substance use problems, including individual and group therapy and medications to help clients reduce alcohol, tobacco, and drug use.
